title: Anthropic sues Trump administration over blacklisting decision : NPR
description: The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
content:
Anthropic filed two federal lawsuits on Monday against the Trump administration alleging that Pentagon officials illegally retaliated against the company for its position on artificial intelligence safety.

title: Anthropic sues Trump administration after Pentagon blacklists AI firm
description: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has filed lawsuits against the Trump administration after the Pentagon designated it a “supply chain risk” amid a dispute over restrictions on military use of its Claude AI chatbot.
content: The artificial intelligence firm Anthropic has filed lawsuits against the administration of Donald Trump, challenging a decision by the United States Department of Defense to classify the company as a “supply chain risk.” The move follows a dispute o... [2925 chars]

title: Trump announces energy pledge with major tech firms to cover AI power costs
description: President Donald Trump announced the Ratepayer Protection Pledge, requiring major tech companies to pay for the electricity used by their artificial intelligence data centers. Companies including Google, Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, xAI, OpenAI and Amazon agreed to build or purchase new power generation to support their expanding AI data centers. The companies will negotiate special power […]

title: OpenAI Buying AI Security Startup Promptfoo to Safeguard Agents
description: OpenAI has agreed to buy Promptfoo, a startup that enables large businesses to find and fix security issues in artificial intelligence models during their development, part of a push to help corporate customers reduce possible risks from deploying AI software.
